note: Per-DIRECTION ratchet floors for PRODUCTION (non-test/fixture) sharded≡exact RESOLVED-edge divergences on the unified resolution model (ADR-0033, supersedes ADR-0032). functionsOnly{Exact,Sharded} MUST be 0 (hard failure). Each direction is gated independently — EXCEEDING any floor fails graph-equivalence-check (a NEW divergence on the unified model); a decrease passes with a tighten hint. Gating per-direction (not a flat total) so a fixed conflict cannot mask a new phantom. Both engines run ONE model: the single-program (exact) build recovers cross-package edges via the SAME boundary linker (resolveCrossBoundaryCalls) the sharded build runs post-merge (exact = the 1-shard case). DIVERGENCE JOURNEY: 204 (pre-plan) -> 12 (Phase 3 convergence) -> 1 (chained-call position fix, this follow-up). RESIDUAL (1, tracked): phantom=1 (sharded resolved, exact declined) — packages/graph/engine/src/cli/dashboard-data.ts:44 `scope.graph?.rules.getAll()`: sharded CORRECTLY resolves to RuleRegistry.getAll; exact under-resolves the method call through the OPTIONAL CHAIN (`?.`) property access. NOT a fabrication — a real edge exact misses. Follow-up: exact optional-chain property-access method resolution. conflict=0: the former 11 conflicts were NOT same-name disambiguation — they were CHAINED-CALL POSITION COLLISIONS (`recv(...).method(...)`: the inner call `recv()` and outer call `recv().method()` share the expression-start column, so two REAL distinct edges collided on one (owner,line,column) identity and the two engines kept different members). FIXED by anchoring a call edge at its CALLEE token (method name / class / callee identifier) via `calleeAnchorNode` (edge-resolvers/syntactic.ts), used by both tsPosition (in-shard) and positionOf (cross-shard) so the engines agree and both real edges survive. decline=0. KNOWN OUT-OF-SCOPE (both engines decline → invisible to this differential gate, guarded by the pinned-corpus resolution-completeness floor in resolution-completeness-floor.test.ts): arrow-property method calls like logger.info() where findCatalogEntry cannot reproduce the arrow occurrence's hash. DEFERRED (explicit): clean-checkout parity for WORKSPACE imports — exact still resolves @scope/pkg through the dep's built dist/*.d.ts to reach the boundary linker; relative imports are clean-safe (file+name pin). Source-as-surface deferred; Phase 6's clean-checkout assertion is scoped to the relative class.
